topic.what: Black-box auditing framework (GateScope) for third-party LLM API gateways to detect undisclosed behaviors and consistency violations
topic.how: Four-dimension analysis: response content fingerprinting, multi-turn conversation consistency, billing accuracy verification, and latency characteristics measurement
topic.why_matters: Commercial LLM gateways operate as opaque intermediaries with undisclosed routing, caching, and billing policies, leaving users unable to verify model authenticity, response integrity, or cost accuracy

characteristics.evidence: Applied to 10 real-world commercial LLM API gateways
characteristics.context: Selection criteria not specified in excerpt

characteristics.what_it_measures: Frequency of silent model downgrading or switching
characteristics.evidence: Uncovered silent model substitutions across tested gateways
characteristics.context: Detailed breakdown not provided in excerpt

characteristics.what_it_measures: Divergence between expected and actual billing costs
characteristics.evidence: Found deviations from announced pricing across platforms
characteristics.context: Includes cached token charges and extra token billing

characteristics.evidence: Degraded memory retention in multi-turn settings
characteristics.context: Related to silent truncation before documented limits

characteristics.evidence: Substantial variation in latency stability across platforms
characteristics.context: Influenced by routing, model switching, and extra processing

novelty: First systematic black-box auditing framework for third-party LLM API gateways
Four-dimension measurement pipeline covering content, conversation, billing, and latency
Structured JSON-schema prompts for stable behavioral signature extraction
Empirical analysis of 10 commercial gateways revealing pervasive undisclosed behaviors
open_problems: Scalability of probing across all model types
Detection of sophisticated gateway evasion techniques
Long-term monitoring frameworks for behavioral drift
